goal: Fix BUG-019: streak-locked deck appearing active for anonymous users after SPA logout
done: Added resetActiveDeck() in js/data.js (3-line export that sets module-level activeDeckId back to DEFAULT_DECK_ID). Extended js/auth.js onAuthStateChange listener to call resetActiveDeck() when _event === 'SIGNED_OUT'. Extended named import on js/auth.js:2 to include resetActiveDeck. Three-line behavior change in auth.js, four-line addition in data.js
symptom: After SPA-style logout (no hard reload), deck-inventory re-renders and shows the previously-selected streak-locked deck (e.g. boba-oracle) as active even though the user has no session. Hard reload self-heals the bug
root_cause: Module-level cache in js/data.js — `export let activeDeckId = getActiveDeckId();` runs once at module load. getActiveDeckId() correctly re-reads cleared localStorage after logout and returns 'moonmallow', but getActiveDeck() reads the cached `activeDeckId` variable, not localStorage. The cache stayed pinned to the pre-logout deck id until the next page reload. Originally-filed root cause (missing localStorage clear) was wrong — that clear was already in place via commit f7817aa1 on 2026-05-14, six days before the bug was filed
fix: Surgical module-cache reset on the canonical SIGNED_OUT supabase event. Placed after authListeners.forEach fanout so subscribers see auth state change first, before maybeShowLoginReward and pendingClaim gates (both of which gate on session?.user so are SIGNED_IN paths)
verified: localStorage clear at js/auth.js:118-131 already in place since 2026-05-14
notes: Belt-and-suspenders defense (re-read localStorage inside getActiveDeck()) was considered and deferred — resetActiveDeck() covers all current paths. canUnlockDeck() remains the single source of truth for the unlock gate; this fix is additive, not a gate replacement. Canonical-source only — www/ and ios/ mirrors deferred to user's cp + cap sync workflow. open-bugs.md BUG-019 entry updated in companion commit with corrected root-cause analysis.
